observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

6. Exhaust all possible remedies for the determination of details of the respective debtors and initiate collection of the long outstanding accounts.

7. Ensure that the retiring employees have already settled their money or property accountabilities before the issuance of an agency clearance.

8. Periodically review the receivables by preparing the Aging Schedule to determine the inactivity of the accounts so as to take appropriate and immediate action thereon.

9.  Consider the applicability of the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008 on the writing-off of the dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management:

12. Require the Accountant to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for each project or activity. Exhaust all possible means in order to substantiate all the balances of the account.

13. Hereafter, require the focal persons to fast-track the implementation of the projects.

14. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the liquidation report and return the savings from completed projects to the source agencies. Henceforth, projects should be fully implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation.

15. Consider the application of the COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 for dormant accounts.

18. We recommended that management conduct verification and validation of the foregoing claims against the LGU to return these balances to the respective contractor/supplier or be forfeited in favor of the LGU in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

19. Also, we recommended that management regularly monitor the claims under Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable for the timely release of retention money and or performance security or forfeiture of the same when necessary.

We recommended that the Management require the MDRRM Council to:

24. Include in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate in the Local Development Plan, under a separate caption, the list of all projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.;

25. Present in the LDRRMFIP the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum and in details the 70% Mitigation Fund.

26. Maintain and submit regularly to COA the reporting requirements in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We reiterate our recommendation that management:

33.	Require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees by withholding or suspension of their salaries;

34.	File Request for Write-off those Advances to Officers and Employees which liquidation is no longer possible such that those of the deceased officials and employees and those separated officials after verification, analysis and validation; and

35. Strictly implement that all cash advances granted within the year should be liquidated at yearend.

We recommend that the Management require the MDRRM Council to:

37.	Include in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate in the Local Development Plan all programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) to be charged to prior years unexpended DRRMF in the special trust fund; and

38.	Present the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum in the LDRRMFIP.

45. We recommended that Management be informed and updated of the relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards like the DILG’s Seal of Good Local Governance and comply with the provisions therein like the submission of PSCP.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the aforementioned errors.
Moving forward, thoroughly review each transaction related to the procurement of construction materials, fuel, and lubricants, ensuring their accurate recording in the books of accounts, in accordance with the guidelines in IPSAS 17.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Make the necessary adjusting entries and furnish the Office of the Auditor with the copy of the journal entry voucher.
Exercise due diligence when recording journal entries and ensure that any adjusting journal entries are made in strict compliance with COA Circular 2015-009.
Evaluate whether these funds are adequately supported by cash/funds for proper budgeting and fund allocation.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Send out demand letters to all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances immediately.
Develop a strategic way to expedite liquidation of cash advances of officers and employees who have already died or no longer in service with the option that after exhausting efforts to have these accounts settled and no other alternative procedures are available, management may file a request for write-off.
Comply with the requirements set forth under COA Circular 2016 - 005 dated December 19, 2016 which provides for the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies, Local Government Units and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations.
Recognize impairment loss to reflect the limited probability of collection of assets.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management facilitate the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE by performing the following:
Identify other departments within the LGU that may have available staff with relevant skills (e.g., record-keeping, accounting, or logistics) who can be temporarily reassigned to assist and focus with the inventory process.
Collaborate with other government agencies that can provide training on inventory management, especially on the full implementation of the 'One-time Cleansing of PPE Accounts,' as outlined in COA Circular 2020-006, so that the LGU may be fully aware of the primary guidelines of the said circular.
Establish a new set of Inventory Committee, with members temporarily relieved of their regular duties until the completion of the physical inventory within a clear and agreed-upon timeline.
Ensure that Inventory Committee to assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.
Ensure that the newly reconstituted Inventory Committee prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking process.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require and ensure the submission of the SWA and the supporting documents required for each transaction, whether by administration or implemented through a contractor; and
Moving forward, ensure that the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the completeness of the supporting documents to ensure the validity, propriety, and legality of the transaction prior to processing and preparation of checks.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant:
1, Together with the various offices inventory custodian and Inventory Committee, conduct physical inventory count of supplies and materials to establish the inventory balance on hand in the books of accounts;
Record all procured supplies and materials initially as inventory and recognize as expenses only upon actual issuances of inventory;
b. Various Offices to:
Assign a designated inventory custodian responsible for tracking supplies and maintaining updated records/reports; and
Implement inventory monitoring report and submit the SSMI to Accounting as basis for recognition of supplies expenses.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management ensures that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO):
Discontinue the implementation of PPAs that do not align with the LCPC's mandate.
Comply with the guidelines set forth in DILG Circular 2012-120, dated July 4, 2012, when planning and implementing the PPAs for LCPC.
Management Comment
The Management commented during the exit conference that they were unaware that the some of their spendings did not align with the priority items specified in the circular. However, they committed to adhere to the recommendations.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the LDRRM Council:
Identify specific disaster-related projects/activities to be charged under the said trust fund; and,
Include in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan, under a separate caption the list of projects and activities to be charged to unexpended balances of the LDRRMF of previous years and incorporate in the Local Development Plan.
Require its designated focal person to attend the relevant training programs highlighting necessary knowledge, skills, and understanding of DRRM policies and reporting requirements to allow for a more effective fulfillment of his responsibilities and proper implementation of disaster risk reduction and management initiatives.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the General Services Office (GSO) in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to take the following actions:
Implement a formal system, manual or electronic for logging daily usage of all heavy equipment with detailed records of equipment operating hours, tasks performed, and locations served.
Designate a staff member or team to be responsible for the daily logging of equipment usage, ensuring consistency and accuracy ensuring that the logs are verified as to their completeness.
Provide training for equipment operators on the importance of proper recordkeeping and the correct procedures for logging usage data.

1. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted and to make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that the transactions affecting these accounts are recorded properly, and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments.

16. We reiterate our recommendation that Management establish an Internal Audit Service (IAS) office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS, and Section 124 of PD 1445.

17. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen the control measures over the cash/collections to prevent accumulation of the undeposited collections in compliance with Section 62 (C) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

20. We recommended that Management direct the BAC and all concerned End-Users to ensure that correct mode of procurement is used in the procurement of office supplies and/or equipment, moving forward.

We recommended that management:

21. Submit a written explanation why the BAC failed to procure the goods in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184.

22. Cease the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts and other related transactions pursuant to COA Circular no. 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government.

23. Advise the BAC to thoroughly deliberate on the appropriate mode of procurement before the LGU acquires the necessary goods. This proactive approach will help prevent similar scenarios from occurring in the future.

We recommended that Management:

27. Immediately revert the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-CO of CY 2023 to the General Fund and make it available for use in the DRRM activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.

28. Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers of the unexpended LDRRMF by year of transfer as well as separate subsidiary ledgers from different sources, as applicable.

We recommend that management direct the following courses of actions:
To the Municipal Treasurer:

29.	Compute the RPT and SET Receivable based on the RPT Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card;

30.	Prepare and submit the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers at the end of the year and ensure that the amount of the delinquent taxes was incorporated in the established receivable amount to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant;

To the Municipal Accountant:

31.	Ensure that the delinquent taxes and the report submitted by the MTO are reconciled in order to achieve the accurate figure to recognize; and

32.	Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and set up the RPT and SET Receivable on the first day of the year.

36. We reiterate our recommendation that Management establish an Internal Audit Service (IAS) office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS, and Section 124 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

39. Review and to determine the source agency of those funds entrusted to the LGU for proper recording of these accounts in the Inter-Agency or Intra Agency Payable account;

40.	Validate and verify the existence of the claims by coordinating the claimant and to gather the perfected contract or any document covering such liability; and

41.	Revert those accounts with no valid claimants and submit the copy of the journal entry voucher to the Office of the Auditor.

42. We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to attend GAD seminars and trainings to capacitate them in the execution of GAD related laws and regulations, including the concept and application of GMEF in determining the LGU’s level of GAD mainstreaming and identifying steps to improve its GAD mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended that management direct the following courses of actions:

To the Municipal Treasurer:

Compute the RPT and SET Receivable based on the RPT Register/ Taxpayer’s Index Card; 

Prepare and submit the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers at the end of the year and ensure that the amount of the delinquent taxes was incorporated in the established receivable amount to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant.

To the Municipal Accountant:

Ensure that the delinquent taxes and the report submitted by the MTO are reconciled in order to achieve the accurate figure to recognize; and

Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and set up the RPT and SET Receivable on the first day of the year.

We recommended that management:

Require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees by withholding or suspension of their salaries; 

File Request for Write-off those Advances to Officers and Employees which liquidation is no longer possible such that those of the deceased officials and employees and those separated officials after verification, analysis and validation; and

Strictly implement that all cash advances granted within the year should be liquidated at yearend.

We recommended that management conduct verification and validation of the foregoing claims against the LGU in order to return these balances to the respective contractor/supplier or be forfeited in favor of the LGU in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184. Also, we recommend that management should regularly monitor the claims under Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable for the timely release of retention moneys and or performance security or forfeiture of the same when necessary.

We recommended that the LGU establish GAD database to contain sex disaggregated data to serve as source of information for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management establish an Internal Audit Service (IAS) office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS, and Section 124 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Management require the MDRRM Council to:

Include in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate in the Local Development Plan all programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) to be charged to prior years unexpended DRRMF in the special trust fund; and

Present the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Review and to determine the source agency of those funds entrusted to the LGU for proper recording of these accounts in the Inter-Agency or Intra Agency Payable account;

Validate and verify the existence of the claims by coordinating the claimant and to gather the perfected contract or any document covering such liability; and

Revert those accounts with no valid claimants and submit the copy of the journal entry voucher to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to attend GAD seminars and trainings to capacitate them in the execution of GAD related laws and regulations, including the concept and application of GMEF in determining the LGU’s level of GAD mainstreaming and identifying steps to improve its GAD mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended that Management be informed and updated of the relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards like the DILG’s Seal of Good Local Governance and comply with the provisions therein like the submission of PSCP.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted and to make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that the transactions affecting these accounts are recorded properly, and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Exhaust all possible remedies for the determination of details of the respective debtors and initiate collection of the long outstanding accounts.
Ensure that the retiring employees have already settled their money or property accountabilities before the issuance of an agency clearance.
Periodically review the receivables by preparing the Aging Schedule to determine the inactivity of the accounts so as to take appropriate and immediate action thereon.
Consider the applicability of the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008 on the writing-off of the dormant accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries;
Validate and verify the existence of each of the claims by coordinating with the creditors, gathering the documents covering such liability, and eventual settlement of the obligations determined to be actually due.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Accountant to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for each project or activity. Exhaust all possible means in order to substantiate all the balances of the account.
Hereafter, require the focal persons to fast-track the implementation of the projects.
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the liquidation report and return the savings from completed projects to the source agencies. Henceforth, projects should be fully implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation.
Consider the application of the COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 for dormant accounts.
Management Comment
Most of this amount consists of a certain project in Barangay Libertad. For now, Management cannot write off the amount as they are waiting to release the wage on labor. We will follow up on the remaining ₱5 million. Rest assured that Management is committed to following the recommendations.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to establish an IAS office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS, and Section 124 of PD 1445.
Management Comment
The HR already recommended to the Municipal Mayor the installation of IAS, however due to PS limitation it wasn’t pushed through. They will consider other ways like designating someone or use contract of service for the mean time.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen the control measures over the cash/collections to prevent accumulation of the undeposited collections in compliance with Section 62 (C) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.
Management Comment
Management commented that they are committed to transparency and accountability in their financial operations and are open to any further inquiries or recommendations from COA to improve our practices. They explained and clarified that the Treasurer’s Office continues to receive collections until the last days of December. However, due to banking holidays and closures during this period, it is not always feasible to deposit these funds into the bank immediately.
Management assures that all collections are properly recorded and safeguarded until they can be deposited in the bank. Management makes it a priority to deposit the funds into the designated bank account during the first week of the following year, as soon as banking operations resume.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to conduct verification and validation of the foregoing claims against the LGU to return these balances to the respective contractor/supplier or be forfeited in favor of the LGU in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommended and Management agreed to regularly monitor the claims under Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable for the timely release of retention money and or performance security or forfeiture of the same when necessary.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC and all concerned End-Users to ensure that correct mode of procurement is used in the procurement of office supplies and/or equipment, moving forward.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit a written explanation why the BAC failed to procure the goods in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184.
Cease the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts and other related transactions pursuant to COA Circular no. 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government.
Advise the BAC to thoroughly deliberate on the appropriate mode of procurement before the LGU acquires the necessary goods. This proactive approach will help prevent similar scenarios from occurring in the future.
Management Comment
Management explained that with regard to the drugs and medicines being procured, the RHU’s budget was separated by program that was why they separate the procurements according to programs. For the Christmas lights, procurements were broken into smaller amounts due to budget insufficiency.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
We urge the Management to consolidate the procurements of similar items then have them procured by lot, and always plan for the procurements to avoid any shortcomings in the future.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to require the MDRRM Council to:
Include in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate in the Local Development Plan, under a separate caption, the list of all projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.;
Present in the LDRRMFIP the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum and in details the 70% Mitigation Fund .
Maintain and submit regularly to COA the reporting requirements in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Immediately revert the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-CO of CY 2023 to the General Fund and make it available for use in the DRRM activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.
Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers of the unexpended LDRRMF by year of transfer as well as separate subsidiary ledgers from different sources, as applicable.

We recommended that management: 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize immediately all of the cost of the distributed seedlings based on the Distribution Report and recognize the corresponding Loans Receivable and Donation account; and 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the office of the auditor together with the copy of the Distribution Report.

We recommended that Management:

Comply with the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 and subsequently submit RPCPPE to the office of the Municipal Accountant;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile RPCPPE with their records and effect necessary adjusting entries when necessary and the copy which shall be furnished to the Office of the Auditor; and

Direct the General Services Officer to prepare and issue the ICS for those items reclassified from PPE to semi-expandable properties.

We recommended that Management:

Not to approve applications for regular monetization of leave credits if the available VL credits are not sufficient.

Require the Human Resource Management Officer to adjust and deduct the monetization of leave credits of the abovementioned monetizations from their respective accumulated VL credits for regular monetization, which is covered by Section 22 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave. For monetization of more than 30 days or of 50% or more, the VL credits should be exhausted first before using the sick leave credits, as prescribed in the CSC Resolution No. 00034 dated January 5, 2000; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly scrutinize the claim of employees who wish to monetize 50% or more of their accumulated leave credits (VL & SL) and require them submit justifiable reasons and additional requirements as provided for under Sec. 23 before payments be made.

We recommended that management require the Accounting Department to ensure that the required and appropriate withholding taxes are deducted before making any payments to local suppliers for the purchase of goods and services in accordance with the above-mentioned provisions and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management ensure that only employees whose attendance is essential and critical to the seminar, symposia, assemblies and the likes to be attended shall be authorized to ensure compliance to the Executive Order No.77.

We recommended that the Municipal Government follow strictly the guidelines on travel under EO No. 77 and the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness, validity and propriety of the supporting documents before payment is made. Further, ensure that payment for lodging during official travels are in accordance with the rates in EO No. 77 and refrain from paying actual expenses for meals following the same guideline.

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that PAPs funded out of the 20% EDF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PAPs that are still unimplemented and ongoing, positive efforts should be made to fast track the implementation of the PAPs;

Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PAPs identified in AIP; and 

Ensure that development projects are well-planned and are procurement-and-implementation-ready.

1.18 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive creating the
Inventory Committee and to implement the guidelines and procedures in
inventory taking, recognition of those found at the station, and disposition for
non-existing and/ missing PPE items for one-time cleansing of PPE as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and specifically to:
a) Prepare and complete the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing
the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-
off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the
inventory taking activities from start, up to the targeted completion
of physical inventory;
b) Submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking
activities;
c) Investigate the discrepancies noted between the balances of PPEs in
the Notes to FS and the list of PPEs in the Schedule of Depreciation;
and
d) Conduct the inventory-taking activities over a three-month period,
starting on July 1, 2022, in accordance with the PIP, ensuring that
all necessary information such as the cost and specific identifiers
like the dates and property numbers are indicated for all properties
of the Municipality.

1.19 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the various inventory items
in the PPE accounts and the accumulated depreciation attributed to
said items;
b.) review previous Disbursement Vouchers to trace the procured
PPEs in prior years and reconcile the same with the Schedule of
Depreciation.

2.13 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare the Subsidiary Ledgers of all bank accounts as well as SLs for
all projects to facilitate easy verification of individual balances; and
b. review and reconcile the YCRRP transactions and exert utmost effort
in gathering all the supporting documents to determine the accurate
unexpended balance; thereafter, submit the Utilization Report of the
project and immediately return to the Source Agency any unused
amount.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the revenues from
the slaughterhouse operation amounting to ₱87,473.65 as Prior Year’s
Adjustment and all subsequent collection therefrom as income of the LGU.
Henceforth, recognize the same as income instead of liability.

4.18 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of
the Municipal Planning Development Council, through the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal
Engineer:
c) Review the viability and necessity of unimplemented projects in the
Annual Investment Plan funded from Current and Continuing
Appropriations and rank said projects by order of priority, urgency, and
impact during the screening process of each budget year; and
d) Reprogram prior years’ projects to major projects that can be
implemented within the specific time schedules during the budget year to
maximize the utilization of the 20% DF.

5.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement
Voucher, and the Municipal Treasurer issue the checks in favor of the
Bureau of Treasury/Source Agencies for the return of the unutilized funds
totaling ₱863,174.23.

6.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, DRRM Officer, Municipal
Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel to
desist from utilizing LDRRMF for administrative expenses and instead,
charge the same to the General Fund appropriations.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the previous
year’s LDRRMF to the bank account of the Trust Fund for Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund;
b. Municipal Accountant to record the receipt of fund in the Trust Fund
book as Trust Liability- DRRM; and
c. MDRRMO Designate to include the unexpended balance of previous
years’ LDRRMF in the preparation of the current year’s Local Disaster
Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan.

8.7 We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Council, through the MDRRMO:
c. incorporate in the LDRRMF Investment Plan the projects, programs and
activities relevant to the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction
and management; and
d. identify specific and time-bound PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan
where expected outputs are readily measurable.

9.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractors who are
still covered within the defects liability period to pay the retention money or
post its valid substitute.

9.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct retention
money from payments to contractors, or require a substitute in the
acceptable form, in future procurements of the LGU to protect the interest of
the Municipality.

10.16 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and the Municipal
Accountant submit the following supporting documents to validate the
eligibility of the claimants, and the accuracy of the amounts paid:
a) List of the frontline HRHs and HWs including the BHWs prepared by
the Local Health Board, duly validated by the Department of Health
(DOH) representative;
b) Appointment of Utility Workers and Barangay Health Workers and
Office Order indicating their duties, respective work schedule, and
place of assignment or station duly approved by the Municipal Mayor;
c) Duly accomplished DTRs of all recipients of the AHDP and SRA, as
evidence of the days that the HWs and HRHs physically reported for
work at their assigned work stations; and
d) Certification of the Municipal Health Officer that the claimants actually
rendered service at the Rural Health Unit or at the LGU’s quarantine
facility.

11.10 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
a) Request from the source agency the actual information on the unit cost
and quantities of the COVID 19 vaccines that the LGU will receive in the
future, and record the same in the appropriate logbook to account the
receipt and utilization of said vaccines; and
b) Provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents relative
to the receipt of the vaccines, including the data on the doses
administered at the end of each month.

11.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record using the
perpetual inventory system, the vaccines that will be received by the LGU
and the doses that will be dispensed, using as reference the report and
documents submitted by the MHO.

1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to
recognize depreciation for all its depreciable PPE accounts, exclude residual
value in the computation of depreciation for Road Networks account, and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to reflect the correct carrying
amount of PPE.

2.8 We recommended that an Inventory Committee be created to conduct
a physical count of inventories and submit a Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor.

2.9 We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant
record purchases of inventories as asset and record the corresponding expense
account only when these inventories are consumed, exchanged or distributed as
required in IPSAS 12.

3.6 We recommended that the officials and employees concerned
immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances and henceforth, refrain
from granting additional cash advances to officials and employees with existing
unliquidated cash advances.

4.10 We recommended that management obtain from the Source Agencies
(SA), copies of the Memorandum of Agreement covering each program/project
funded out of fund transfers. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare Fund Utilization Report and record liquidation for each fund transfer.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the guidelines
on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF as provided for in DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

6.14 We recommended that the:
a) Municipality discontinue utilizing its LDRRM Fund for
items/activities that are not related with the four thematic areas of
disaster risk reduction and management;
b) Municipal Accountant prepare journal entry voucher to transfer
the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund;
c) Municipal Treasurer make the actual transfer of cash from the
General Fund to the Trust Fund bank account of the LGU; and
d) Municipal Budget Officer prepare and maintain separate registries
for LDRRM Fund.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipality ensure the approval of their
ten-year MSWMP by the NSWMC. Henceforth, observe the timely
preparation, review and submission of its SWMP.

8.8 We recommended that the:
(a) Municipality immediately implement the Revised Local Revenue
Code in the ensuing year;
(b) Accounting and Budget Offices coordinate in the treatment of
various revenue accounts and preparation of reports;
(c) Local Finance Committee propose a reasonable income to be
collected for the ensuing year, taking into account prior years’
experience as aide in setting the targets; and
(d) Conduct periodic review of collection performance and implement
corrective measures to fully attain projections during the year.

9.11 We recommended that the Municipality limit its disbursements up to
the amount that was actually appropriated and the HRMO to prepare a
database of contractual/JO personnel and review their functions and duties in
order to determine the appropriate number of personnel of an office.

10.10 We recommended that henceforth, the Municipality use the DPRI in
setting the ceiling price of drugs and medicines to be procured following the
guidelines prescribed by DOH Administrative Order No. 2015-0051 dated
December 17, 2015, with supplemental guidelines, and DILG Memorandum
Order No. 2017-06 dated January 16, 2017.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal GAD Focal Person ensure the
maximum utilization of the GAD Funds through the implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects in order to attain the objectives for which the
funds were provided.

12.6 We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant remit in full and intact all taxes
withheld, GSIS and Pag-IBIG premium contributions and loan amortizations
within the period prescribed in Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-012, RA
8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 and RA 9679 or HDMF Law of 2009, respectively.

We recommend that management should require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, immediately deposit intact all collections/cash on hand and submit justification for his failure to deposit collections daily and intact. Henceforth, ensure that all collections are deposited in accordance with Section 307 of RA 7160 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same, together with the bank statements, credit/debit memos and paid checks. Henceforth, strictly observe the prescribed period of its submission as provided for in COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the OIC Municipal Accountant, to close one of the depository accounts with the same purpose and those which are no longer active or needed.

We recommend that the management submit at the beginning of the year to COA a list of on-going and to be implemented project/program/activities for the current year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive propose to the Sangguniang Bayan, for the creation of positions and appropriation for salaries and wages of its personnel, as well as other budgetary requirements of the office.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not appropriate for salary of LDRRMO in CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management Board or Focal Person be required to facilitate the establishment of a sanitary landfill site pursuant to Republic Act 9003 or the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.”
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to exert earnest efforts to intensify collections in order to generate more income for the Municipality. Likewise, look into the cause/s of the decrease in income in order to improve collections.

We recommend that the MDRMO prepare and submit to the audit team a reliable report of utilization of the LDRRMF; and utilize the LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities which conform to the objective of the fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for each type of enterprise and prepare the trial balances and financial statements in accordance with existing regulations to report on the financial condition and results of operations of each enterprise.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agricultural Office to submit written explanation/justification: a. why a project study and the guidelines for the operation of the project and for the selection of beneficiaries were not prepared/submitted; b. on the basis for the selection of Engr. Costelo as the beneficiary of the project and project site; and why the deliveries did not pass thru the review and inspection of the municipal inspectorate; not all materials were used in the construction of the building and the perimeter fencing; livestock delivered were not in accordance with the PO; c. why MOA between the Municipal Mayor and the beneficiary was not prepared; d. why monitoring reports were not prepared. Further, the Municipal Mayor should also direct the BAC Chairman to submit written explanation/justification why: a. the canvass was not evaluated by the BAC; b. the BAC Resolution recommending alternative mode of procurement not approved by HOPE.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local
road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly adhered to, thus
Management should:
a) Comply with the transitory provision in recording local roads in the
books of account;
b) Recognize depreciation expense for the Road Networks assets; and
c) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System. Likewise, enjoin the General Services
Officer to maintain Local Road Network Property Card, and Local
Road Inventory and Road Map, and render a Report on Local Road
Network.

We recommended that the accounting personnel be required to faithfully
perform their duties pursuant to Section 444, Sub-section b.1, paragraph x
of RA 7160. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit
bank reconciliation statements (BRS) together with the copies of bank
statements, cancelled and paid checks, debit/credit memos and all other
supporting documents necessary to facilitate the verification of monthly
BRS.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant
be required to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in
accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular
No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal
Agriculturist be required to fast-track the implementation of programs and
projects in accordance with the timelines upon which the projects should be
implemented or completed.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator be required to implement as planned, the
projects funded under the 20% development fund, in order to attain the
desired purpose of the projects and enable the constituents to enjoy its
benefits.

We recommended that the Management comply with Section 21 of R.A 10121
in the allocation of the required minimum amount to be set aside for LDRRM
Fund in order to have sufficient funds in carrying out its disaster risk
reduction and management programs, projects and activities, and attain the
objective of the law.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board be required to:
(a) facilitate the establishment of a sanitary landfill site; (b) strictly
implement the “No Segregation, No Collection Policy” at source; and (c)
request the augmentation of funds for the purchase of lot to be used as
sanitary landfill in compliance with the requirements of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to intensify
collection in order to meet, if not surpass the targeted amount of Real
Property Tax Revenue. Likewise, require the Municipal Assessor to provide
the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant with the assessment roll. On the
other hand, require the Municipal Accountant to set and record tax
receivables based on valid data base or documents.

We recommended that henceforth, Management execute a Contract of
Service or Memorandum of Agreement when hiring Job Order (JO)
personnel, stating therein the duties and responsibilities, expected outputs
and the rate per day of the workers.

We recommended that management submit to the Auditor a status report on
the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations embodied in
the CY 2017 Annual Audit Report (AAR) using the prescribed form under
COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 dated March 18, 2014. They shall likewise
furnish a copy of the said report to DBM, the Speaker of the House of
Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House
Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either
in printed form or by way of electronic document, as mandated by Section
93 of RA 10924.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) prepare
Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR), and furnish the COA Audit Team
with a copy thereof, to serve as an effective tool in the systematic and logical
assessment/evaluation of the efficiency and effectiveness of the
Municipality’s Procurement System.

We recommended that management allocate at least 5% of the total annual
budget for the implementation of GAD related activities as required under
Section 36(a) of RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women. Also, require
the GAD Focal Point System to prepare and submit the GAD Plan and
Budget / GAD Accomplishment Report to DILG Provincial Office in
accordance with PWC-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.
2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

1. We recommend that the PPE Local Chief Executive create an inventory and committee, who shall take charge of the annual inventory-taking and to submit a report thereon. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: PPE Schedules Property Cards are being prepared. The Inventory Committee has yet to prepare the PPE Physical Inventory.

observations.recommendation:
2. Analyze the accounts with abnormal or negative balances and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the account balance.
3. Prepare subsidiary ledger for each account and maintain individual employee ledger cards for purposes of recording amounts withheld from employees’ salaries and subsequent remittances thereof to the proper government agencies, in order to easily detect occasions of over and under remittances.
observations.managementAction: OIC-Municipal Accountant to be required to: On-going reconciliation of the inter-agency accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management exert extra efforts to invite applicants to the position of a Municipal Accountant and consider the immediate hiring of a qualified personnel, if any, to perform the mandatory accounting functions under Section 474 of RA 7160. Meanwhile, require the designated Municipal Accountant to attend COA trainings/seminars on financial or accounting matters.
observations.managementAction: A permanent Municipal Accountant was hired effective January 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should strictly comply and include in the Invitation to Bid to be posted in the PhilGEPS, all the data/information required in Section 21.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to attract more bidders who will participate in the competitive bidding that would result to procurement of the municipality with the lowest price and best quality.

We recommend for the establishment of a Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and require the Municipal Officer to appropriate at least one percent (1%) of their IRA for LCPC programs, projects, and activities, pursuant to DILG Memorandum dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend that management create or establish a GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) who will prepare and submit the GAD Plan and Budget, as well as the Accomplishment Report, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted that they have created GAD Focal Point and submitted GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer maintain/update his Cashbook and henceforth reconcile with the accounting records regularly.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer implemented cashbook but not immediately updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a copy of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. Not all approved POs and contracts were furnished to the Auditor within 5 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the full remittance of the above RPT shares and barangay aids to the concerned barangays in order to prevent its use for other purpose in the operation of the municipality and to enable the barangays the immediate appropriation of the funds for projects/programs beneficial to the constituents. Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT collections should be remitted regularly within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Six out of eight barangays of the municipality have already received their RPT shares.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned local officials refrain from budgeting and charging non priority expenditures against the Special Education Fund as provided for under Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998 of DBM and DILG. Likewise, the Chairman and other Local School Board officials should prioritize projects and activities that address the needs for quality education of its constituents.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause the LDRRMO to prepare and submit a Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to the COA Auditor of the LGU, copy furnished the OCD and the DILG-LGOO.

We recommend that management ensure the existence of a valid Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement to serve as a guide in the implementation of the project. Also, we recommend that the BAC submit all supporting documents to support the claims for the projects and to facilitate our evaluation of its implementation status. Likewise, we recommend the computation of liquidated damages for every day of delay starting from the last day of implementation which is December 26, 2016 up to the present at 1/10 of 1% per day.
observations.managementAction: A Notice of Suspension 17-001-101(16) dated 7/10/17 was issued. Management submitted some of supporting documents which are still subject for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the preparation of the PPMP. It is useful to require the department heads to submit the PPMP simultaneously with the time they submit their respective budget proposals. The PPMP could be part of their budget submission for consideration by the Sanggunian Bayan. The Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman ensure that the required PPMP are consolidated in the APP to be made as basis before any procurement of goods and services are undertaken to properly plan and monitor the priority activities of the Municipality for a particular budget year. Management should strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 7.2 up to 7.3.4 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in the preparation and submission of PPMP for inclusion in the proposed budget and consolidation with the APP.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The BAC promised to adhere with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere strictly to the provisions of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC, the Municipal Supply Office – Designate or the Municipal Treasurer and the BAC Secretariat to ensure that provisions of RA 9184 are strictly observed in the procurement of goods and services.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. The BAC promised to adhere with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the formation of the Municipal Waste Management Board that shall prepare, submit and implement a plan for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under geographic and political coverage as prescribed in Section 12 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents are complete before signing box A of the DVs. Prepare the supporting documents checklist of supporting documents which are still subject for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. A Notice of Suspension 17-001-101(16) dated 7/10/17 was issued. Management submitted some of supporting documents which are still subject for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management shall: (a) stop the practice of deducting against salaries of municipal officials and regular employees any amount for contribution for remittance to private financial institutions other than those allowed under Sec. 37 of GAA of 2013 and take into consideration the minimum net take home pay of P4,000.00 when effecting salary deductions in consonance with the provisions of the 2013 GAA; (b) exercise control and prudence before allowing employees to avail of loans particularly those from private lending institutions.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause the liquidation of outstanding cash advances and disapprove the grant of additional cash advance to all officers and employees who have outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 dated May 18, 2009. Likewise, the Accountant should refrain from processing cash advances to officers and employees who failed to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. He should also cause the withholding of the AO’s salary equivalent to the unliquidated amount.
observations.reason: Unliquidated Cash Advances was reduced.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should: 32. Direct the BAC Chair and its members, the BAC Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG) to carefully study the provisions of R.A. 9184 and issuances of the GPPB, and to implement and enforce the same in all procurement of the municipality; 33. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly check all supporting documents before releasing the voucher for payment; and 34. Designate an employee, duly authorized by the BAC, who will conduct the canvass through shopping or small value procurement.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. The BAC and Municipal office promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly check all supporting documents before releasing the voucher for payment; and designate an employee to conduct canvass.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer immediately maintain/update his Cashbook and henceforth reconcile with the accounting records regularly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has issued Executive Order No. 1 requiring the OIC_Mun. Treasurer to update his cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the MDRMO prepare and submit to the audit team a reliable report of utilization of the LDRRMF; and Utilize the LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities which conform to the objective of the fund.

We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to the COA Auditor of the LGU, copy furnished the OCD and the DILG-LGOO.

We recommend that management ensure the existence of a valid Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement to serve as a guide in the implementation of the project. Also, require the BAC submit all supporting documents to support the claims for the projects and to facilitate our evaluation of its status of implementation. Likewise, we require the computation of liquidated damages for every day of delay starting from the last day of implementation which is December 26, 2016 up to the present at 1/10 of 1% per day.

We recommend that: a) Management sets its internal guidelines on the preparation of the PPMP. It is useful to require the department heads to submit the PPMP simultaneously with their respective budget proposals. The PPMP could be part of their budget submission for consideration by the Sangguniang Bayan. b) The Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman ensure that the required PPMP are consolidated in the APP to be made as basis before any procurement of goods and services are undertaken to properly plan and monitor the priority activities of the Municipality for a particular budget year. c) Management strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 7.2 up to 7.3.4 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in the preparation and submission of PPMP for inclusion in the proposed budget and consolidation with the APP.

We recommend that the LCE direct the formation of the Municipal Waste Management Board that shall prepare, submit and implement a plan for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under geographic and political coverage as prescribed in Section 12 of RA 9003.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit/deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required in Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to report immediately to the LCE any undeposited collection discovered while recording the transaction in the books of accounts, for appropriate action.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for each type of enterprise and prepare the trial balances and financial statements in accordance with existing regulations to report on the financial conditions and results of operations of each enterprise.

We recommend that management shall: (a) stop the practice of deducting against salaries of municipal officials and regular employees any amount of contribution for remittance to private financial institutions other than those allowed under Sec. 37 of GAA of 2013 and take into consideration the minimum net take home pay of P4,000.00 when effecting salary deductions in consonance with the provisions of the 2013 GAA; (b) exercise control and prudence before allowing employees to avail of loans particularly those from private lending institutions.
observations.reason: Management explained that they cannot just delete the deductions because they have outstanding Memorandum of Agreement with Creditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Agricultural Office to submit written explanation/justification: 1. why a project study and the guidelines for the operation of the project and for the selection of beneficiaries were not prepared/submitted; 2. on the basis for the selection of Engr. Costelo as the beneficiary of the project and project site; and why the deliveries did not pass thru the review and inspection of the municipal inspectorate; not all materials were used in the construction of the building and the perimeter fencing; livestock delivered were not in accordance with the PO; 3. why MOA between the Municipal Mayor and the beneficiary was not prepared; and 4. why monitoring reports were not prepared. Further, the Municipal Mayor should also direct the BAC Chairman to submit written explanation/justification why: 1. the canvass was not evaluated by the BAC; and 2. the BAC Resolution recommending alternative mode of procurement not approved by HOPE.
observations.reason: For further verification because this occurred in previous administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory
committee, who shall take charge of the annual inventory-taking and to submit
a report thereon. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare the PPE ledger cards and property cards,
respectively.

We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be required to:
a) Analyze the accounts with abnormal or negative balances and prepare
the necessary adjusting entries to correct the account balance.
b) Prepare subsidiary ledger for each account and maintain individual
employee ledger cards for purposes of recording amounts withheld
from employees’ salaries and subsequent remittances thereof to the
proper government agencies, in order to easily detect occasions of over
and under remittances.

We recommend that management should require the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer to immediately deposit intact all collections/cash on hand and submit
justification for his failure to deposit collections daily and intact. Henceforth,
ensure that all collections are deposited in accordance with Section 307 of RA
7160 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be required to
prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same, together
with the bank statements, credit/debit memos and paid checks. Henceforth,
strictly observe the prescribed period of its submission as provided for in COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. Likewise, instruct the Municipal
Treasurer, in coordination with the OIC Municipal Accountant, to close one of
the depository accounts with the same purpose and those which are no longer
active or needed.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly
observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of
RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management exert extra efforts to invite applicants
to the position of a Municipal Accountant and consider the immediate hiring of
a qualified personnel, if any, to perform the mandatory functions provided
under Section 474 of RA 7160. Meanwhile, require the designated Municipal
Accountant to attend COA trainings/seminars on financial or accounting
matters.

We recommend that management submit at the beginning of the year a list
of on-going and to be implemented project/program/activities for the current
year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate
project signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should
strictly comply and include in the Invitation to Bid to be posted in the PhilGEPs,
all the data/information required in Section 21.1 of the Revised IRR of RA
9184 in order to attract more bidders who will participate in the competitive
bidding that would result to procurement of the infrastructure and goods
with the lowest price and of best quality to the advantage of the Municipal
Government of Tunga, Leyte.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive propose to the Sangguniang
Bayan, for the creation of positions for the LDRRM Office and appropriation for
the salaries and wages of its personnel, as well as other budgetary requirements
of the office.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board or the Focal
Person be required to facilitate the establishment of a sanitary landfill site
pursuant to Republic Act 9003 or the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act
of 2000.”

We recommend for the establishment of a Local Council for the Protection
of Children (LCPC) and require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at
least one percent (1%) of their IRA for LCPC programs, projects, and activities,
pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and
Assessor to exert earnest efforts to intensify collections in order to generate more
income for the Municipality. Likewise, look into the cause/s of the decrease in
income in order to improve collections.

We recommend that management create or establish a GAD Focal Point
System (GFPS) who will prepare and submit the GAD Plan and Budget, as well
as the Accomplishment Report, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer refrain from the practice of signing the checks in advance to prevent the incurrence of unauthorized expenditures/disbursements and he should be primary liable on the acts have done wrong by himself and likewise the Disbursing Officer is also liable who prepared the checks even the Municipal Treasurer is absent.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was reprimanded and discontinued the practice of signing checks in advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive provide a safe receptacle to the Accountable Officers in the Municipal Treasurer's Office who have a custody of cash and accountable forms and ensure protection of government funds and properties.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Treasurer was provided with a steel vault and the office was protected against intrusion by unauthorized persons.

observations.recommendation: Create an inventory committee to conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE by type, in order of establishing the correctness of the reported balances as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal assets and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records; and Prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger cards and the inventory report. Likewise, obtain copies of the tax declarations and verify the inclusion of all land in the inventory list against the tax declarations obtained and act on any discrepancy noted.
observations.managementAction: A committee was created and an inventory was conducted; however, the result of the inventory was not prepared and reported in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE).
observations.reason: A committee was created and an inventory was conducted however, the result of the inventory was not prepared and reported in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE). Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management attend procurement training and updating the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The participants attended trainings and seminars sponsored by DBM R.O. VIII.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a copy of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from the perfection/approval.
observations.reason: Not all approved P.O.s and contracts were furnished to the Auditor within 5 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the full remittance of the above RPT shares and barangay aids to the concerned barangays in order to prevent its use for other purpose in the operation of the municipality and to enable the barangays the immediate appropriation of the funds for projects/programs beneficial to the Barangay constituents. Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT collections should be remitted regularly within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Six Barangays out of 8 barangays of the municipality have already received their RPT shares.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned local officials refrain from budgeting and charging non priority expenditures against the Special Education Fund as provided for under Section 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG. Likewise, the Chairman and other Local School Board officials should prioritize projects and activities that address the needs for quality education of its constituents.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the Comparison of Budget and Actual Amount presented for each level of legislative oversight in accordance with PPSAS including its reconciliation and maintain the required registries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant submitted the Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Engineer-Designate and the members of the Municipal Engineering Office and the Technical Working Group of the BAC familiarize themselves with the promulgations issued by the DPWH particularly for infrastructure projects in order to avoid over computation of ABC.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of the Estimated work items in the Program of Work were calculated in accordance with DPWH Department Order No. 22 dated February 18, 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management ensure strict compliance with RA 10121 and other rules and regulations on the creation of MDRRMO through enactment of a Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance, as required.

We recommend management ensure timely implementation of the project in order to have timely attainment of project objectives to intended beneficiaries and not to deprive the constituents of the availment of benefits which could have been enjoined. Likewise, we also recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and other Officers concerned to adopt monitoring system for the efficient and effective implementation of their development plans, programs and priorities.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC, the Municipal Supply Office – Designate or the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that provisions of RA 9184 are strictly observed.
observations.reason: Laxity on the procurement process, however the BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct officials responsible in the preparation of the 2014 Municipal Budget to adhere strictly with the provision of Section 325 of RA 7160, specifically in the computation of PS limitation. Also, LGU’s Annual Budget should be thoroughly reviewed first before approval.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed officials in the preparation of the 2014 Municipal Budget to strictly adhere to the provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents are complete before the signing box A of the DVs. Prepare checklist of supporting documents in every particular transaction to ascertain completeness of the attachment in the DVs.
observations.reason: A Notice of Suspension 17-001-101(16) dated 7/10/17 was issued. Management provided some of the supporting documents which are still subject for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor should cause the liquidation of outstanding cash advances and disapprove the grant of additional cash advance to all officers and employees who have outstanding cash advances and to take appropriate action pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 dated May 18, 2009.
observations.reason: Unliquidated Cash Advances was reduced to P187,675.21.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 are strictly followed in the establishment and utilization of Petty Cash Fund. He should also direct the Accountant not to process replenishment of PCF without the necessary documents. Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) should be adequately supported with documentary requirements to prove the validity of payment.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should: 1. Direct the BAC Chair and its members, the BAC Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG) to carefully study the provisions of R.A. 9184 and issuances of GPPB, and to implement and enforce the same in all procurement of the municipality; 2. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly check all supporting documents before releasing the voucher for payment; and 3. Designate an employee, duly authorized by the BAC, who will conduct the canvass through shopping or small value procurement.
observations.reason: The BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: a) Management sets its internal guidelines on the preparation of the PPMP. It is useful to require the proposed department heads to submit the PPMP simultaneously with other departments or end users with their corresponding PPMPs. b) The Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman ensure that the required PPMP are consolidated in the APP to be made as basis before any procurement of goods and services are undertaken to properly plan and monitor the priority activities of the Municipality for a particular budget year. c) Management strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 7.2 up to 7.3.4 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in the preparation and submission of PPMP for inclusion in the proposed budget and consolidation with the APP.
observations.reason: Still the management was not able to consolidate PPMPs into the APP because of non-submission of the corresponding PPMPs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive form a committee that will facilitate and complete the conduct of physical count of all properties of the Municipality and prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment within the one month from the completion of the inventory-taking.

We recommend that the LCE direct the formation of the Municipal Waste Management Board that shall prepare, submit and implement a plan for safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under geographic and political coverage as prescribed in Section 12 of RA 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the 2016 Annual Audit Report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct officials responsible in the preparation of the Municipal Budget to adhere strictly with the provision of Section 325 of RA 7160, specifically in the computation of limitation. Also, LGU’s Annual Budget should be thoroughly reviewed first before approval.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the 2016 Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit/deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required in Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to report immediately to the LCE any undeposited collection discovered while recording the transaction in the books of accounts, for appropriate action.
observations.reason: Still the Municipal Treasurer failed to deposit collections intact within the prescribed period. A reconciliation activity will be done.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Audit Committee to facilitate and complete the conduct of physical count of all properties of the Municipality and prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.reason: Reiterated in the 2016 Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: (i) Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE from General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (Code 438). However, all unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for Capital Outlay (CO) should be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded thereof are completed. (ii) Provide proper disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE. The unutilized balance should be recognized and used in the disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP to be spent in the succeeding five years.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for each type of enterprise and prepare the trial balances and financial statements in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the 2016 Annual Audit Report.
