observations.recommendation: We recommend that management shall: (a) stop the practice of deducting against the salaries of municipal officials and regular employees any outstanding contribution for remittance to private financial institutions other than those allowed under Sec. 37 of the GAA of 2013 and take into consideration the minimum net take home pay of P4,000.00 when effecting salary deductions in consonance with the provisions of the 2013 GAA; (b) exercise control and prudence before allowing employees to avail of loans particularly those from private lending institutions.
observations.reason: For further verification because this occurred in previous administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agricultural Office to submit written explanation/justification: 1. why a project study and the guidelines for the operation of the project and for the selection of beneficiaries were not prepared/submitted; 2. on the basis for the selection of Engr. Costelo as the beneficiary of the project and project site; and why the deliveries did not pass thru the review and inspection of the municipal inspectorate; not all materials were used in the construction of the building and the perimeter fencing; livestock delivered were not in accordance with the PO; 3. why MOA between the Municipal Mayor and the beneficiary was not prepared; and 4. why monitoring reports were not prepared. Further, the Municipal Mayor should also direct the BAC Chairman to submit written explanation/justification why: 1. the canvass was not evaluated by the BAC; 2. the BAC Resolution recommending alternative mode of procurement not approved by HOPE.
observations.reason: For further verification; occurred in previous administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Treasurer immediately maintain/update his
Cashbook and henceforth reconcile with the accounting records regularly.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer refrain from the practice of signing
the checks in advance to prevent the incurrence of unauthorized
expenditures/disbursements and he should be primary liable on the acts have done
wrong by himself and likewise the Disbursing Officer is also liable who prepared the
checks even the Municipal Treasurer is absent.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive provide a safe receptacle to the
Accountable Officers in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to safeguard their
specially who have a custody of cash and accountable forms and ensure protection
of government funds and properties.

We recommend that management:
a) Create an inventory committee to conduct an annual physical inventory of
PPE by type, in order of establishing the correctness of the reported balances
as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal assets
and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable
reconciliation with the accounting and property records; and
b) Prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with
the general ledger cards and the inventory report. Likewise, obtain copies of
the tax declarations and verify the inclusion of all land in the inventory list
against the tax declarations obtained and act on any discrepancy noted.

We recommend that management attend procurement training and seminars duly
conducted by the GPPB’s Regional Composite Team.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and/or
Disallowance, as evidence warrants.

We recommend that Management submit a copy of contracts, purchase orders
and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five (5)
working days from perfection/approval.

We recommend the full remittance of the above RPT shares and barangay aids to
the concerned barangays in order to prevent its use for other purpose in the
operation of the municipality and to enable the barangays the immediate
appropriation of the funds for projects/programs beneficial to the Barangay
constituents. Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT collections should be
remitted regularly within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of
RA 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned local officials
refrain from budgeting and charging non priority expenditures against the Special
Education Fund as provided for under Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Joint Circular
No. 1 s. 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG. Likewise, the Chairman and other Local
School Board officials should prioritize projects and activities that address the
needs for quality education of its constituents.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where warranted.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
to submit the Comparison of Budget and Actual Amount presented for each level
of legislative oversight in accordance with PPSAS including its reconciliation
statement and maintain the required registries. Henceforth, see to it that all
Obligation Requests are duly accomplished before processing this document and
the Disbursement Voucher.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer-Designate adhere strictly with
DPWH D.O. No. 22 dated February 18, 2015 in the preparation and calculation
of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC). Likewise, the members of the
Municipal Engineering Office and the Technical Working Group of the Bids and
Awards Committee familiarize themselves with the promulgations issued by the
DPWH particularly for infrastructure projects.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND if warranted.

We recommend that the management:
a) Require the MDRRMO to prepare and submit to the audit team a reliable
report of utilization of the LDRRMF; and
b) Utilize the LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities which conform to
the objective of the fund.

We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after end of each
month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to the COA
Auditor of the LGU, copy furnished the OCD and the DILG-LGOO.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where warranted upon complete
audit of the transactions.

We recommend Management to ensure strict compliance with RA 10121 and other
rules and regulations on the creation of MDRRMO through the enactment of a
Sangguniang Bayan Ordinance, as required.
We recommend that the LCE direct the Accountant to see to it that the financial statements will be submitted on time or before February of the ensuing year, as required in COA Circular No. 2010-01.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.reason: The BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC, the Municipal Supply Office – Designate or the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Laxity on the procurement process, however the BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Chairman, Bids and Award Committee, the Supply Officer-designate and the Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Laxity on the procurement process, however the BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Chairman, Bids and Award Committee, the Supply Officer-designate and the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Laxity on the procurement process, however the BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly with the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and COA Circulars.
observations.reason: Laxity on the procurement process, however the BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to follow strictly the documentary requirements embodied in COA Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to prioritize and consider the filling-up of vacant positions necessary for the proper discharge of the municipality’s functions, targets and programs; b) Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of its functions, targets and programs.

The Local Chief Executive should: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to submit accounts and reports for all funds promptly in strict adherence to Section 347 of RA 7160 and other relevant provisions; b. Direct the municipal treasurer to attend to supplies and property concerns and consider designating another official to manage supplies and property; c. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain registries of appropriations, allotments and obligations; d. Consider timely and complete submission of reports in granting performance-based benefits to employees.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor should: a. Direct the immediate supervisor of the driver or the employee concerned to comply with the rules and regulations prescribed in the Manual for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles under COA Circular No. 77-61. b. Require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the documentary requirements are submitted first before processing the payment and that the prescribed Driver’s Trip Ticket is used. c. Ensure monthly report of fuel consumption of government vehicles is submitted.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents are complete before signing box A of the DVs and prepare a checklist of supporting documents for every transaction.
observations.reason: The LGU submitted some of supporting documents which are still subject for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes from the honorarium and professional fees paid to officials of NGAs and LGUs in accordance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 and require claimants to refund the unwithheld tax.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause the liquidation of outstanding cash advances and disapprove the grant of additional cash advance to all officers and employees who have outstanding cash advances pursuant to the provision in COA Circular 97-002 dated May 18, 2009. Likewise, the Accountant should refrain from processing cash advances to officers and employees who failed to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. He should also cause the withholding of the AO’s salary equivalent to the unliquidated amount.
observations.reason: Cash Advances was reduced to P187,675.21.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 are strictly followed in the establishment and utilization of Petty Cash Fund. He should also direct the Accountant not to process replenishment of PCF without the necessary documents. Disbursement Vouchers should be adequately supported with documentary requirements to prove the validity of payment.
observations.reason: Some regular expenses were still charged to PCF and replenishment deficiencies addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should: 1. Direct the BAC chair and its members, the BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group (TWG) to carefully study the provisions of R.A. 9184 and issuances of the GPPB, and to implement and enforce the same in all procurement of the municipality; 2. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly check all supporting documents before releasing the voucher for payment; and 3. Designate an employee, duly authorized by the BAC, who will conduct the canvass through Shopping or Small Value Procurement.
observations.reason: The BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.
